page: Microsoft Azure D16lds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Dldsv7 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is provisioned with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes a 945 GB local storage disk and supports Premium Storage.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor features advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (VNNI and BF16) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8). In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, achieving 32.42 GB/sec in gzip compression and 13.98 GB/sec in bzip3 decompression. Multi-core stress-ng CPU performance registers at 15943.77, while the Passmark CPU score is 26945.67, indicating solid mid-range compute capabilities.

The instance provides 124.77 GB/sec of large-block read memory bandwidth and 53.03 GB/sec of write bandwidth. For database and web serving workloads, it delivers 27.33 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 30.27 GB/sec. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, such as web serving, build systems, and lightweight machine learning inference, where its AMX instructions assist in achieving 267.04 tokens/sec for small LLM text generation.
description: The Microsoft Azure D16lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor clocked at 4.2 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 945 GB local storage disk. With a 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is designed for compute-intensive tasks that require moderate memory. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which contribute to strong single-threaded performance in compression benchmarks and efficient small-scale LLM text generation. The server is well-suited for workloads such as web serving, database operations, and compute-heavy applications that benefit from dedicated CPU resources and local storage without the overhead of high memory configurations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16lds_v7 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, designed for high-compute workloads with moderate memory needs.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16lds_v7 is a 16-vCPU Intel Xeon server with 32 GB RAM and 945 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual server featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel processors, integrated local storage, and advanced matrix extension capabilities.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
32.0 GB system memory providing a 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Suitable for web serving, database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference
